about.title: About Novus Epoxy
about.subtitle: Your premium surface coatings distributor — Worldwide delivery

story.title: Our Story
story.p1: Novus Epoxy was born from the belief that every surface deserves an exceptional coating. Founded in Quebec, our company is the result of a passion for high-performance materials and a strategic partnership with SCI Coatings, a Montreal-based manufacturer recognized for over 15 years.
story.p2: Our mission is clear: to make industrial-grade coatings accessible to every project, whether residential, commercial, or industrial. From your garage to your factory floor, we bring the same level of excellence.
story.p3: Our model is simple: we select the best products, provide impeccable technical support, and deliver directly from the factory to your job site, anywhere in the world.

partnership.title: SCI Coatings Partnership
partnership.subtitle: Proudly Made in Montreal
partnership.description: SCI Coatings Inc., based in Anjou (Montreal), is a Canadian leader in manufacturing industrial surface coatings since 2011. Their state-of-the-art facilities produce systems tested and certified for the most demanding environments.
partnership.description2: As an exclusive distributor, Novus Epoxy gives you direct access to the full SCI range — from residential to heavy industrial — with personalized technical support.

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: The experts behind every successful project

jason.name: Jason Lanthier
jason.bio: A visionary entrepreneur, Jason founded Novus Epoxy with the ambition of making premium coatings accessible worldwide.

michael.name: Michael
michael.bio: With over 15 years of experience in chemical formulation, Michael oversees R&D and ensures the quality of every SCI system.

luca.name: Luca
luca.bio: An operational strategist, Luca optimizes every process to ensure a smooth and efficient client experience.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: We deliver throughout Quebec in 3-5 business days. For the rest of Canada, allow 5-10 days. International shipping is available by air and sea.
faq.a2: Yes! We have a network of certified installers across Quebec. We can also train your team for autonomous application.
faq.q3: How long do your coatings last?
faq.a3: Our SCI systems are designed to last 15-25 years depending on the coating type and usage. All our products are guaranteed and laboratory tested.
faq.q4: How do I get a quote?
faq.a4: Fill out our online quote form or call Jason directly at 581-307-2678. You'll receive a detailed estimate within 24 hours, no obligation.

curingConditions.title: Your Curing Weather
curingConditions.subtitle: Check if conditions are right for applying your epoxy coating. Real-time temperature, humidity, and precipitation data.
curingConditions.statusDesc_ideal: Temperature, humidity, and precipitation are all within optimal ranges. You can apply your coating with confidence.
curingConditions.statusDesc_caution: Conditions are borderline. Consult a specialist before proceeding with application.
curingConditions.statusDesc_unfavorable: It's too cold, too humid, or rain is expected. Postpone application to a more favorable day.

installation.processSubtitle: From consultation to curing, we handle everything

0.title: Consultation
0.description: Free on-site assessment. We evaluate your surface, discuss your needs, and recommend the ideal system.

1.title: Surface Prep
1.description: Professional diamond grinding or shot-blasting to create the perfect concrete profile for adhesion.

2.title: Application
2.description: Multi-layer system applied by our certified crew using professional-grade equipment and techniques.

3.title: Curing
3.description: Controlled curing process. Most systems are walk-ready in 24h and vehicle-ready in 72h.

installation.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about our installation services

0.question: How long does a typical installation take?
0.answer: Most residential installations (garages, basements) are completed in 1-2 days. Larger commercial projects may take 3-5 days depending on the system selected and surface area. Polyurea systems offer the fastest turnaround with 24-hour return to service.

1.question: Do I need to prepare anything before installation?
1.answer: Simply clear the area of all items and ensure access to power and water. Our team handles all surface preparation, including grinding, crack repair, and cleaning. We'll provide a detailed checklist when you book your installation.

2.question: What temperature is required for installation?
2.answer: Ideal application temperature is between 10 and 30 degrees Celsius with humidity below 80%. Our team monitors conditions closely and can use fast-cure formulations in borderline conditions. Use our free Curing Conditions tool to check your local weather.

3.question: How soon can I use my floor after installation?
3.answer: Light foot traffic is typically possible after 24 hours. Vehicle traffic is recommended after 72 hours. Full chemical resistance is achieved after 7 days of curing. Polyurea systems achieve full cure even faster.

4.question: Do you offer warranties on installations?
4.answer: Yes. All professional installations include a full manufacturer warranty on materials plus our installation workmanship guarantee. Specific warranty terms vary by system — contact us for details on your chosen system.

5.question: Do you service areas outside of Quebec?
5.answer: Yes! While our primary installation team is based in Quebec, we have certified applicators across Canada. For DIY installations, we ship products nationwide and provide full technical support. Contact us with your location for availability.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about our epoxy, polyurea, and quartz coating systems.
faq.noResults: No results found. Contact us for more information.
faq.ctaDescription: Our team is available to answer all your questions and guide you to the best system for your project.

q1.question: How much does an epoxy floor cost per square foot?
q1.answer: Prices range from $2.75 to $11.00 per square foot depending on the system chosen. The SCI-100 Coating starts at $2.75/sqft, the Flake system at $2.85/sqft, Polyurea Flake at $4.50/sqft, Metallic at $5.50/sqft, and Cementitious Polyurethane can reach $11.00/sqft. Request a free quote for an exact price tailored to your project.

q2.question: Do you offer volume discounts?
q2.answer: Yes. We offer progressive discounts on all systems: 5% from 1,000 sqft, 10% at 2,500 sqft, 15% at 5,000 sqft, and 20% at 10,000 sqft and above. Additionally, our pallet pricing offers extra savings of 14-18% compared to individual unit purchases.

q3.question: What is the current promotion?
q3.answer: In April 2026, we are offering 20% off all SCI coating systems. This promotion is valid until April 30, 2026 and applies to all products, including epoxy, polyurea, quartz, and metallic systems. Combinable with volume discounts.

q4.question: How long does an epoxy floor last?
q4.answer: A professional Novus Epoxy epoxy floor lasts between 10 and 20 years depending on the system, traffic, and maintenance. Quartz systems like the SCI-Quartz Broadcast have a 15+ year lifespan even in very high-traffic areas. Cementitious polyurethane systems are the most durable with 20+ years of useful life.

q5.question: Is epoxy resistant to chemicals?
q5.answer: Yes. All our systems offer chemical resistance. For aggressive industrial environments (acids, solvents), we recommend the SCI-Slurry ($6.00-$9.00/sqft) or the SCI-CPU cementitious polyurethane ($7.00-$11.00/sqft) which provide superior-grade chemical resistance.

q6.question: How do you maintain an epoxy floor?
q6.answer: Maintenance is simple: sweep regularly and mop with a neutral cleaner and warm water. Avoid abrasive products and high-pressure cleaners directly on the surface. For stubborn stains, use a mild non-acidic cleaner. No additional wax or sealant is needed.

q7.question: Can a damaged epoxy floor be repaired?
q7.answer: Yes. Minor chips and scratches can be repaired locally without redoing the entire floor. For more significant damage, partial resurfacing is possible. Contact us to evaluate the necessary repair -- we can provide touch-up products compatible with your existing system.

q8.question: Do you ship across Canada and internationally?
q8.answer: Yes. Novus Epoxy ships across Canada, the United States, Europe, and worldwide via air and sea freight. All our products are manufactured by SCI Coatings in Montreal, Quebec. Delivery within Canada typically takes 3-7 business days, and 7-14 days for international.

q9.question: What are the shipping costs?
q9.answer: Costs vary depending on the destination and order weight. Pallet orders benefit from competitive rates through our carrier agreements. Contact us for a precise shipping quote included in your free estimate.

q10.question: What is the difference between epoxy and polyurea?
q10.answer: Epoxy (starting at $2.75/sqft) offers excellent chemical resistance and a glossy finish, but requires 24-72h cure time. Polyurea ($4.50-$7.00/sqft) cures in 2-4 hours, is 4x more flexible, and UV resistant. The SCI-Polyurea system allows complete installation in 1 day -- ideal for projects requiring rapid return to service.

q11.question: Which system do you recommend for a residential garage?
q11.answer: For a residential garage, we recommend the SCI-Flake system ($2.85-$4.75/sqft) or the SCI-Polyurea Flake ($4.50-$7.00/sqft). The Flake offers excellent value with a decorative finish. The Polyurea Flake adds superior resistance to thermal shock and de-icing salts -- perfect for Canadian winters.

q12.question: Can you install epoxy yourself (DIY)?
q12.answer: Novus Epoxy sells directly to professionals and property owners. Our SCI products are professional-grade with detailed instructions. One gallon of SCI-100 ($89) covers 80-120 sqft. For best results, we recommend installation by a certified professional -- concrete preparation is critical for long-term adhesion.

q13.question: What are the ideal conditions for applying a coating?
q13.answer: The ideal temperature is between 10C and 30C with humidity below 80%. The concrete must be dry (moisture test < 4%), clean, and free of contaminants. Use our curing conditions tool on novusepoxy.ca to check if conditions are favorable in your area in real time.

q14.question: How long does installation take?
q14.answer: Time varies depending on the system and surface area. A standard garage (400-600 sqft) takes 2-3 days with a classic epoxy system (including preparation, primer, base coat, and sealer). The SCI-Polyurea system can be completed in 1 day thanks to its rapid 2-4 hour cure time between coats.

q15.question: Do you offer a warranty on your products?
q15.answer: All SCI Coatings products are covered by a manufacturer warranty. Duration varies by system: residential systems are warranted for 5-10 years, and commercial/industrial systems for 3-5 years. The warranty covers manufacturing defects when the product is applied according to the provided technical specifications.

q16.question: What is the process for getting a quote?
q16.answer: It's simple and free: 1) Fill out the online quote form with your project details (surface area, usage type, desired system). 2) Our team contacts you within 24h with a detailed estimate. 3) You approve and we schedule delivery. You can also use our online price calculator for an instant estimate.

q17.question: Are SCI products manufactured in Canada?
q17.answer: Yes. All coating systems are manufactured by SCI Coatings Inc. in Anjou, Montreal (Quebec). SCI is a Canadian manufacturer specializing in high-performance floor coatings for over 15 years. Novus Epoxy is the exclusive brand distributor, with worldwide shipping.

installationGuide.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to know to prepare, apply, and maintain a professional epoxy, polyurea, or quartz coating.
installationGuide.section1Subtitle: Surface preparation is the most critical step. Poorly prepared concrete is the #1 cause of delamination and coating failure.

0.title: Visual Inspection
0.description: Inspect the entire surface for cracks, expansion joints, oil, grease, old paint, efflorescence, or any contaminants. Note damaged areas that need repair before grinding.
0.tip: Perform the water drop test: if water beads instead of being absorbed, a sealer or contaminant is blocking adhesion.

1.title: Diamond Grinding
1.description: Mechanical grinding with diamond segments to create the CSP 2-3 surface profile required by the manufacturer. This step removes concrete laitance and opens pores for optimal primer penetration.
1.tip: Use an industrial HEPA vacuum to capture concrete dust during grinding.

2.title: Defect Repair
2.description: Repair all cracks with a flexible epoxy repair compound. Fill holes and pitting with a leveling mortar. Ensure repairs are level with the surrounding surface.
2.tip: Allow repairs to dry 12-24 hours before applying primer.

3.title: Thorough Cleaning
3.description: Degrease the surface with an alkaline cleaner. Rinse thoroughly with clean water. Vacuum all residual dust with an industrial vacuum. The surface must be perfectly clean and dry.
3.tip: Never use muriatic acid — it harms epoxy adhesion.

4.title: Moisture Testing
4.description: Perform a concrete moisture test using the calcium chloride method or in-situ probe. Moisture levels must be below 4% for most systems. Excessive moisture causes outgassing and blistering.
4.tip: Concrete temperature minimum: 10°C (50°F) and at least 3°C above dew point.

installationGuide.section2Subtitle: Each system requires a precise multi-layer process. Respect recoat windows between each layer.

0.title: Primer Coat
0.description: The penetrating epoxy primer seals the concrete, prevents outgassing, and creates an adhesion bridge for the base coat. Apply by roller or squeegee.

details.1: Recoat window: 8-16 hours depending on product

1.title: Base Coat
1.description: The base coat is the main body of the system. Apply at the thickness specified by the technical data sheet. Thickness varies from 10 to 40 mils depending on the system.

details.0: Mix Part A and Part B at the exact ratio
details.1: Pot life: 20-45 minutes depending on temperature

2.title: Broadcast / Decorative Finish
2.description: Depending on the system, apply vinyl flakes, quartz aggregate, or metallic pigments into the still-wet base coat. For full broadcast, saturate the surface uniformly.

details.0: Flakes: broadcast to rejection for full coverage
details.1: Metallic: manipulate with a notched squeegee for 3D effect

3.title: Top Coat
3.description: The protective top coat seals the system and provides UV, chemical, and abrasion resistance. Use a polyaspartic or polyurethane depending on the chosen system.

details.0: Apply 2 coats of top coat for optimal protection
details.1: Anti-slip optional: add micro-aggregate to the final coat

installationGuide.section3Subtitle: Compare cure times, thicknesses, and prices for all 14 SCI coating systems. Data directly from technical data sheets.
installationGuide.tableDisclaimer: * Cure times are at 23°C (73°F) and 50% relative humidity. Lower temperatures extend cure times. Prices in Canadian dollars.
installationGuide.fastCureDescription: The SCI-Polyurea ($4.50–$7.00/sq ft) and SCI-GAR ($4.25–$6.50/sq ft) systems offer 2-4 hour cure times and return to traffic in 8-24 hours. Complete installation in 1 day — ideal for garages and businesses that can't shut down for long.
installationGuide.section4Subtitle: Following these conditions is essential for coating performance and durability. Never apply outside these parameters.

0.title: Temperature
0.description: Both ambient AND concrete temperature must be above 10°C (50°F). Ideally between 15°C and 25°C. Above 30°C, pot life decreases significantly.

1.title: Humidity
1.description: Relative humidity must be below 80%. High humidity slows curing and can cause amine blush (whitish haze) on epoxy coatings.

2.title: Ventilation
2.description: Ensure adequate ventilation during and after application. Open doors and windows. Use fans to accelerate evaporation of residual solvents.

conditionsDoList.0: Check the weather 48h before application with our curing conditions tool
conditionsDoList.1: Measure concrete temperature with an infrared thermometer — not just air temp
conditionsDoList.2: Wait until concrete is at least 3°C (5°F) above dew point
conditionsDoList.3: Plan application early morning when temperature is rising (concrete warming up)

conditionsDontList.0: Never apply if rain is expected within 24 hours
conditionsDontList.1: Don't apply late in the day when temperature drops (condensation risk)
conditionsDontList.2: Don't heat concrete artificially — it creates moisture through condensation
conditionsDontList.3: Don't ignore dew point — it's the #1 cause of field failure

installationGuide.conditionsCta: Check curing conditions in your area
installationGuide.section5Subtitle: These common mistakes are responsible for 90% of coating failures. Learn to avoid them for a professional result.

0.title: Skipping Surface Preparation
0.description: The #1 cause of failure. Applying a coating on unground, contaminated, or wet concrete invariably leads to delamination within months.
0.solution: Solution: CSP 2-3 diamond grinding is mandatory. No shortcuts.

1.title: Ignoring Concrete Moisture
1.description: Moisture trapped under the coating creates vapor pressure that causes blisters and peeling. Slabs-on-grade are most at risk.
1.solution: Solution: Mandatory moisture test. Rate < 4%. Use an epoxy vapor barrier if needed.

2.title: Not Respecting Recoat Windows
2.description: Applying the next coat too early traps solvents and creates a soft cure. Too late creates inter-coat adhesion failure.
2.solution: Solution: Follow the technical data sheet exactly. Typical window: 12-24h at 23°C.

3.title: Wrong Mix Ratio
3.description: An incorrect A:B ratio produces incomplete cure — the coating stays soft, tacky, or never hardens. An irreversible error requiring complete removal.
3.solution: Solution: Measure precisely. Use calibrated buckets. Mix for 3 minutes minimum.

4.title: Applying in Cold or Humid Weather
4.description: Below 10°C, most epoxies don't cure properly. High humidity causes amine blush (whitish, powdery surface).
4.solution: Solution: Wait for ideal conditions. Use our curing conditions tool.

installationGuide.recsSubtitle: Our three most popular systems for a successful first installation

0.name: SCI-Flake System
0.description: Our most popular. Decorative flakes on epoxy base. Hides imperfections, non-slip finish. Ideal for garages and basements.

1.name: SCI-Polyurea Flake System
1.description: 1-day installation. Ultra-fast cure, 4x more flexible than epoxy. UV resistant. Perfect when time is limited.

2.name: SCI-Metallic System
2.description: Spectacular 3D effects. Each installation is unique. The premium choice for spaces where aesthetics matter.

installationGuide.ctaSubtitle: Our certified applicators guarantee a flawless result. Free consultation, no obligation.
installationGuide.ctaTrust: Free consultation — Certified applicators — Full warranty — Response within 24h
